lever: Column::reindex_promote_float64_by_optional_positions (fp-columnar): typed null-introducing positional gather for all-valid Int64/Float64 sources, replacing per-row values().get().cloned() + cast_scalar_owned + Column::new in fp-join reindex_outer_join_column
scenario: NEW perf_profile outer_join / left_join: 100000 rows, card 512, 50% key overlap (right keys offset by 256), 2 iters
profile_evidence: perf record on outer_join: reindex_outer_join_column iterator chain 18.8% self+children + ~10% _int_free_chunk (Scalar vec churn); outer was ~5.3x worse per output row than inner, left ~2.6x

paired_hyperfine_outer_join.caveat: host load ~37-51 during measurement (concurrent workloads); alternating same-load per-iter runs agree: before 1712-1807 -> after 778-805 ms/iter (3 consistent pairs, sink identical)

left_join_check: parity (820-1952ms noise band overlaps both binaries; left join routes through its own dense builder, not reindex_outer_join_column) — no regression
mechanism: eager path materializes the typed source column to Vec<Scalar> (values()), clones a 32B Scalar per row, casts each through cast_scalar_owned, collects a new Vec<Scalar>, and Column::new re-runs validity scans; typed path gathers as_i64_slice/as_f64_slice -> Vec<f64> + validity words -> from_f64_values_with_validity (lazy nullable Float64)

isomorphism.floating_point: Int64->Float64 is `v as f64`, the exact cast_scalar_owned Int64->Float64 arm; Float64 source is identity
isomorphism.missing_slots: 0.0 datum + invalid bit materializes Scalar::Null(NullKind::NaN) == missing_for_dtype(Float64) from the eager path (established aligned_binary_f64 gap convention); all-valid sources carry no NaN so every matched slot is valid
isomorphism.fallbacks: nullable/non-numeric sources return None -> unchanged Scalar path; no-missing positions still take reindex_by_positions; non-Int64/Float64 dtypes early-return as before
isomorphism.sink_check: outer sink=19731200, left sink=19631360 identical before/after

harness_addition: perf_profile gains left_join/outer_join scenarios + goldens (50% overlap via build_join_frame_offset) — left/outer joins previously had NO bench coverage
